essence: Supports workplace health by conducting screenings, monitoring employee wellbeing, and assisting occupational health professionals.

identity.title: Occupational Health Technician
identity.summary: Carries out health screenings, monitors workplace exposures, and supports occupational health advisors in promoting employee wellbeing and safety.

salary.notes: Technicians working in high‑risk industries or with advanced testing responsibilities can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Training in occupational health or workplace wellbeing
Ability to conduct tests such as audiometry, spirometry, and vision checks
Understanding of workplace hazards and exposure monitoring

lifestyle.description: Occupational health technicians work across offices, factories, warehouses, and industrial sites conducting health screenings and monitoring workplace exposures. The role suits individuals who enjoy practical health‑related tasks, supporting wellbeing, and contributing to safer workplaces.

habits: Maintains accurate medical and exposure records
Supports occupational health advisors and nurses
Communicates clearly with employees and managers
Monitors workplace hazards and wellbeing indicators
